beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make coffee and instant ramen
beats.reasoning: Night owl wakes late on Tuesday; yesterday's energy at 50/100 plus late library session means a slower morning. Fold wake-up and breakfast routine into one beat.
Steady rain 7am–2pm means Jin walks in wet conditions; drizzle is manageable for a short walk from U-District to campus. Budget 25 min for wet/slow pace.

beats.activity: Attend Data Structures lecture and catch up on problem sets in classroom
beats.reasoning: Jin is a CS student; morning class fits after a late wake-up. Staying on campus to work before lunch avoids a redundant commute in the rain.

beats.activity: Grab lunch indoors at campus food court—pho and an energy drink
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady; indoor meal on campus is practical. Energy drink aligns with Jin's caffeine habit; pho is warm and comforting.

beats.activity: Study in Allen Library, work on assignments
beats.reasoning: Rain ends by 2pm; Jin is already on campus and focused. Library work is core to his routine; extends until mid-afternoon when rain clears.

beats.activity: Walk to IMA for pickup basketball
beats.reasoning: Rain has cleared by 2pm; short walk across campus to the gym. 30 min buffer for post-study transition and getting ready.

beats.activity: Pickup basketball game and shooting around
beats.reasoning: Jin's regular outlet for energy and clearing caffeine buzz; consistent with yesterday's pattern. Weather is clear by afternoon, making courts available.

beats.activity: Walk back to U-District, grab a quick snack
beats.reasoning: Post-exercise wind-down; short walk back to neighborhood. Budget 30 min for post-game cool-down and transition home.

beats.activity: Dinner at Taneda Ramen, sit alone and decompress
beats.reasoning: Repeats yesterday's grounding ritual—comfort food and quiet time. Fits Jin's evening routine and neighborhood proximity to home.

beats.activity: Return home, study in dorm, browse games and Discord, prepare for late-night library session
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down at home before the night-owl library grind. Jin decompresses with low-energy activities (games, social media) before heading back out to study.

beats.activity: Study session at Allen Library, work on Data Structures and other coursework
beats.reasoning: Night owl pattern: Jin heads to library late evening for focused work. Extended session fits his typical 2am library habit; ends well after midnight but before dawn.

beats.activity: Walk home from library through quiet U-District streets
beats.reasoning: Late-night walk home after library close; 30 min for tired, slower pace in dark/quiet streets.
Final beat: Jin sleeps ~3.5 hours before waking late again. Ends at 05:45, well before 06:00 cutoff. Sleep beat spans from Tuesday night into Wednesday morning.

diary: Woke up to grey skies and the sound of rain pattering against the window. Made instant ramen while scrolling through Discord—apparently the 520 bridge was a mess this morning, but I didn't need to cross it. Campus was quiet, just the usual drizzle walk through U-District. Data Structures lecture felt dense; I caught up on problem sets afterward in the classroom, trying to actually understand the material instead of just cramming it later. Grabbed pho at the food court and spent a couple hours in Allen Library before heading to IMA. The basketball court was packed—nice to move around, shoot some hoops, feel like I exist outside my laptop for a bit. Hit up Taneda Ramen solo afterward, letting the warmth of the bowl settle me down. Evening blurred into studying back at the dorm, then another library run to push through more coursework. The rain had stopped by the time I walked home, and the U-District felt peaceful and empty. Tired but kind of satisfied, like I actually made progress today.
thought_process: The rain made me want to stay indoors, so I front-loaded my day with campus time—knocked out lecture and problem sets early rather than spreading them thin. The 520 closure didn't affect me directly since I'm already U-District-based, but it made me grateful for that. I needed the basketball break badly; sitting in the library for hours straight was draining, and moving my body reset my focus. I chose Taneda for dinner specifically to decompress alone—needed that quiet moment. The evening library session happened because I wasn't feeling tired yet and wanted to build momentum on Data Structures before it piles up. Overall, the grey weather kept me grounded and focused; no urge to wander off-campus or get distracted. Just a steady, productive day.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
